diagnosticUse: Strongest OH band. Primary indicator of water vapor dissociation in plasma. Rotational temperature of gas can be extracted by fitting the rotational structure. Present in virtually all atmospheric pressure plasmas containing any moisture.
plasmaType: DBD, APPJ, arc, flame, microwave atmospheric plasma

diagnosticUse: Second OH band. Used with (0,0) band for vibrational temperature measurement.
Blue-shifted OH band. Useful when 306nm region is obscured by other emission.
Intense N₂ second positive band. Standard marker for nitrogen and air plasma. Rotational temperature measurable. Ratio with 337nm band gives vibrational temperature.
plasmaType: DBD, glow discharge, spark, lightning, air plasma

diagnosticUse: Strongest N₂ second positive band. Most commonly observed N₂ emission in all discharge types. First band to appear as plasma ignites. Used as intensity reference for all other N₂ bands.
plasmaType: All nitrogen and air plasma types

diagnosticUse: Vibrational satellite of 337nm band.
Second vibrational satellite.
Used with (0,0) band at 337nm to determine vibrational temperature of N₂.

Primary N₂⁺ emission band. Indicates ionization of N₂. Intensity ratio I(391)/I(337) is a direct measure of reduced electric field E/N in non-equilibrium plasma. Critical diagnostic for streamer and nanosecond pulsed discharge studies.
plasmaType: Streamer discharge, nanosecond pulsed plasma, corona, high-voltage DBD
reference: Pai et al., Plasma Sources Sci.Technol. 2010

diagnosticUse: Vibrational satellite of 391nm N₂⁺ band.
reference: Pai et al., Plasma Sources Sci.Technol. 2010

diagnosticUse: Primary NO emission band in UV. Indicates NO production from N₂+O₂ reactions in plasma. Important for biomedical plasma (NO is biologically active), environmental plasma, and plasma-assisted combustion.
plasmaType: Atmospheric pressure plasma with air/N₂+O₂ mix, plasma agriculture, biomedical APPJ

diagnosticUse: Secondary NO band for vibrational analysis.

Primary CH radical emission. Indicates presence of hydrocarbon fragments in plasma. Used in plasma CVD diamond deposition monitoring, plasma cracking of methane, and combustion diagnostics. Gas temperature measurable from rotational structure.
plasmaType: Methane plasma, CVD reactor, hydrocarbon arc, plasma reforming, combustion

diagnosticUse: Secondary CH band. Confirms CH presence when A-X system is obscured.

Most prominent C₂ Swan band. Bright green emission characteristic of carbon arc plasma. Indicates C₂ dimer formation from carbon ablation or hydrocarbon dissociation. Vibrational temperature measurable from band sequence. The green color of carbon arc lamps comes from this band.
plasmaType: Carbon arc, laser ablation of carbon, carbon nanotube synthesis plasma, comet tails

diagnosticUse: Blue Swan band. Used with 516nm for vibrational temperature of C₂.
Red Swan band sequence member.
Vibrational sequence member.

Strongest CN emission band. Indicates CN radical formation from nitrogen reacting with carbon-containing species. Appears whenever carbon and nitrogen coexist in plasma. Rotational temperature measurable. Important for plasma-assisted nitrogen fixation research and carbon nitride film deposition.
plasmaType: N₂ plasma with carbon electrodes, plasma nitrogen fixation, carbon nitride deposition

diagnosticUse: UV CN band. Used when visible range is congested with other emission.
